tasks_completed.description: Rewrote scripts/execute-push.py to implement the 13-step atomic push protocol per BA spec iter3
tasks_completed.changes: Complete replacement of prior stub (which was missing --branch arg, had wrong step order — push.sh check after grant validation instead of before, re-read HEAD twice instead of single drift check at step 9, no branch field in CLI). New implementation: (1) CLI args: --repo-hash, --branch, --remote, --request-id, [--auto]; (2) SESSION_ID from CLAUDE_SESSION_ID env exit 2 if unset; (3) grant path computed from _SENTINEL_BASE/repo_hash/session_id/request_id.json; (4) push.sh existence+executable check BEFORE any grant access, exit 2; (5) grant file read, exit 1 if absent; (6) JSON parse, exit 1 if malformed; (7) validate expires_at (tz-aware), nonce, branch, remote_name, session_id, verdict, risks — exit 1, grant unconsumed; (8) verdict blocked: print risks stderr, exit 1, grant unconsumed; verdict warn: print risks stderr, flush, continue; (9) single HEAD drift check git rev-parse HEAD vs grant.head_sha, exit 1 if mismatch, grant unconsumed; (10) os.unlink(grant_path), exit 1 on failure; (11) write sentinel atomically mkstemp+os.replace, exactly 5 keys; (12) set CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID, flush stderr; (13) os.execv, on OSError unlink sentinel exit 2. Script is chmod 0755.
tasks_completed.rationale: The prior stub lacked --branch arg, had wrong step order, and would fail AC10 (missing --branch in invocation). The root cause (missing enforcing script for the exec pattern) is now addressed.

tasks_completed.description: Updated commands/push.md Steps 4 and 5 to delegate to execute-push.py exclusively
tasks_completed.changes: Step 4: replaced body with 'execute-push.py (Step 5) is the sole grant validator and consumer. The orchestrator MUST NOT manually read, validate, or unlink the push-analyst grant file.' Step 5: replaced old invocation 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py --request-id "$REQUEST_ID" --repo-hash "$REPO_HASH" --remote "$RESOLVED_REMOTE"' with tilde-form invocation including --branch: 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py --repo-hash "${REPO_HASH}" --branch "${BRANCH}" --remote "${RESOLVED_REMOTE}" --request-id "${REQUEST_ID}"'. Added explanatory note with corrected operation order prose.
tasks_completed.rationale: AC10 requires tilde form with --branch; AC9 requires MUST NOT sole handler language; old form had /root/.claude/ absolute path and was missing --branch.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — missing artifact, not a regression. The exec pattern was designed in task 20260519-211515 but scripts/execute-push.py was never created as an enforcing artifact.
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: The bash block in commands/push.md Step 5 was illustrative pseudocode, not an executable artifact. Without a dedicated Python script, orchestrators fell back to the && pattern (python3 validate_sentinel.py && bash push.sh) which has the timing window.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Creates the missing enforcing script with os.execv semantics — sentinel write and push.sh sentinel-read occur within the same PID lifetime, eliminating all three timing attack surfaces (sentinel mtime aging, grant replay window, state substitution window).

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: The 13-step protocol is inherently sequential with distinct error paths per step. Each of the 10 abort conditions (exit 1 or exit 2) requires its own error message and return path. The BA spec mandated all 13 steps with exact semantics. The minimum diff for a correct, spec-compliant implementation cannot be smaller without omitting required validation steps.

dev.qa_notes: Run AC1-AC10 per acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-push.json. Key behavioral checks: (1) AC1 happy path: valid grant, approved verdict, mock push.sh with MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH, HOME=<tmpdir> isolation — verify sentinel written, grant consumed, push.sh exec'd with CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID set. (2) AC2: non-head_sha field mismatch or expired grant → exit 1, no sentinel, grant preserved. (3) AC3: head_sha mismatch → exit 1 at step 9, no sentinel, grant preserved. (4) AC4: blocked verdict → exit 1, no sentinel, grant NOT consumed. (5) AC5: warn verdict → risks on stderr, sentinel written, grant consumed, push.sh exec'd. (6) AC6: push.sh absent (HOME=<tmpdir> without push.sh) → exit 2, grant preserved. (7) AC7: execv OSError (bad shebang) → exit 2, sentinel cleaned up, grant consumed. (8) AC8: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID unset → exit 2. (9) AC9/AC10: grep tests on commands/push.md. IMPORTANT: All ACs use HOME=<tmpdir> isolation for push.sh lookup; mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh; MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH env for capture; NEVER write to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh.

tests: AC1-AC10 as specified in acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-push.json — test files are a separate cycle concern per out_of_scope_observations in context JSON

blast_radius_declarations.reason: commands/push.md is documentation — AC9 and AC10 verify its content with grep checks; no automated test file required

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 7 findings: 1 BLOCKER (executable bit not set on execute-push.py), 4 MAJORs (branch drift check not in step 9, naive expires_at TypeError risk, path-bearing CLI inputs unconstrained, push.md Step 5 prose operation order wrong), 2 MINORs (handoff message flush, sentinel mkdir in try block). Classifications: BLOCKER applied; MAJOR-expires_at applied (tzinfo check added); MAJOR-prose applied (Step 5 prose corrected); MINOR-flush applied (print flush=True + BrokenPipeError guard). MAJOR-branch-drift classified out_of_scope: spec explicitly says 'single HEAD drift check at step 9 ONLY' using SHA not branch name; branch mismatch is caught in step 7 field validation. MAJOR-path-inputs classified out_of_scope: spec does not require format validation of repo_hash/session_id/request_id — this is defense-in-depth beyond the requirement. MINOR-mkdir-in-try classified out_of_scope: current error handling is functionally correct per the spec.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied 4 findings: (1) chmod 0755 execute-push.py; (2) expires_at tzinfo+utcoffset check with astimezone(utc) normalization and TypeError in except tuple; (3) print flush=True with BrokenPipeError guard on handoff message; (4) push.md Step 5 prose reworded to correct operation order. Rejected 3 findings as out_of_scope: branch-name drift check (spec says single HEAD SHA check only), CLI input format validation (not in spec requirements), sentinel mkdir placement (style preference, functionally correct).

self_verification.notes: python3 -m py_compile execute-push.py: SYNTAX OK. chmod 0755 applied. AC9 grep checks: MUST NOT present, no manual unlink in code blocks, execute-push.py referenced. AC10 grep checks: tilde form with --branch/--request-id/--repo-hash/--remote present, old /root/.claude/ form absent, no &&.*push.sh pattern.

recommendations: Add hooks/tests/test_execute_push.py in a separate cycle to cover AC1-AC8 programmatically (noted in out_of_scope_observations in context JSON)
Consider adding format validation of repo_hash (^[0-9a-f]{16}$) in a future hardening cycle — Codex finding, classified out_of_scope for this cycle
